key_insight: Schedule-free gradient methods can achieve optimal worst-case convergence in smooth nonconvex optimization, while also avoiding strict saddles after a tiny perturbation.
engineering_relevance: This supports using schedule-free optimizers in machine learning pipelines to reduce learning-rate tuning without sacrificing theoretical convergence guarantees.
method: Lyapunov analysis from the continuous-time limit of schedule-free dynamics
Worst-case convergence proofs for schedule-free gradient descent and stochastic gradient descent
Nonautonomous dynamical-systems argument for strict-saddle avoidance
evidence_signal: The paper provides theory-only worst-case analyses and rate guarantees, rather than experimental evaluation, for smooth possibly nonconvex objectives.
watch_item: The guarantees rely on the standard method form and smoothness assumptions, so performance may differ if the optimizer is modified or the objective is not sufficiently smooth.
paper_title: Understanding Schedule-Free Methods in Nonconvex Optimization: Rate Guarantees and Escaping Saddles
